2606.25400.title: BrainAgent: A Large Language Model-Driven Multi-Agent Framework for Autonomous Brain Signal Understanding
2606.25400.paper_summary: Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) and brain signal understanding are pivotal for clinical health and next-generation interactions. Despite this significance, its widespread adoption in real-world scenarios remains restricted, primarily because current analytical paradigms lack sufficient agentic intelligence. First, existing methodologies impose prohibitive technical barriers, requiring extensive specialized expertise. Second, they remain inherently static and task-specific, failing to execute the complex, long-horizon workflows essential for real-world deployment. To accelerate the democratization of brain signal understanding, we draw inspiration from Large Language Models (LLMs) to introduce BrainAgent, an LLM-driven multi-agent framework designed to ground abstract natural language intent into rigorous, executable, and end-to-end processing pipelines. BrainAgent employs a hierarchical architecture where a central supervisor orchestrates specialized sub-agents for adaptive task decomposition and execution. Furthermore, we establish a comprehensive, systematic benchmark for evaluating agentic systems in brain signal analysis. Empirical results demonstrate that BrainAgent effectively automates complex workflows with superior reliability, marking a paradigm shift toward democratized brain signal understanding.
2606.25400.paper_summary_zh: 腦-電腦介面（BCIs）和腦信號理解對於臨床健康和下一代互動至關重要。儘管這一重要性，實際場景中的廣泛採用仍然受到限制，主要是因為當前的分析範式缺乏足夠的代理智能。首先，現有的方法論施加了禁止性的技術障礙，需要廣泛的專業知識。其次，它們本質上是靜態的和特定任務的，無法執行對於實際部署至關重要的複雜長期工作流程。為了加速腦信號理解的民主化，我們受到大型語言模型（LLMs）的啟發，推出了BrainAgent，一個基於LLM的多代理框架，旨在將抽象的自然語言意圖轉化為嚴謹、可執行的端到端處理管道。BrainAgent採用層次架構，其中中央監督者協調專門的子代理進行自適應任務分解和執行。此外，我們建立了一個全面的系統基準，用於評估腦信號分析中的代理系統。實證結果表明，BrainAgent能有效自動化複雜工作流程，並具有更高的可靠性，標誌著向民主化腦信號理解的範式轉變。
2606.25400.author: Yangxuan Zhou et.al.
2606.25400.authors: Yangxuan Zhou, Sha Zhao, Jiquan Wang, Shijian Li, Gang Pan
